messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Build a dashboard module with auth and an API, with unit tests and database stubs. Include modules A and B.
Clarifications:
None
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Build a dashboard module with auth and an API, with unit tests and database stubs. Include modules A and B.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "project-setup",
      "title": "Project setup and scaffolding",
      "description": "Initialize repository, CI/CD pipelines, linters, dependency management, and the basic project scaffolding for the dashboard and API.",
      "status": "completed",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Repository created with initial commit, CI configured, linters running, and a runnable project skeleton that starts without errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "design-api-spec",
      "title": "Design API specification and interfaces",
      "description": "Define REST API endpoints, request/response schemas, authentication flows, and interfaces for the Dashboard, Module A, and Module B in an OpenAPI spec.",
      "status": "in_progress",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-setup"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "OpenAPI (or equivalent) spec committed, reviewed, and signed off; endpoint contracts documented for frontend/backends and testing."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-auth",
      "title": "Implement authentication and authorization",
      "description": "Implement authentication middleware, token issuance/validation, role-based access control hooks, and integrate with API routes.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "design-api-spec"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "Auth middleware enforces protected routes, tokens validated, and auth integration tests demonstrate correct allow/deny behavior."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-module-a",
      "title": "Develop Module A backend",
      "description": "Implement Module A controllers, services, route handlers, and business logic according to the API spec and secure them with auth hooks.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "design-api-spec",
        "implement-auth"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Module A endpoints implemented, return the expected responses for happy and error paths, and pass unit tests with stubs."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-module-b",
      "title": "Develop Module B backend",
      "description": "Implement Module B controllers, services, and route handlers, ensuring integration with shared services and compliance with the API spec.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "design-api-spec",
        "implement-auth"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Module B endpoints implemented and verified via unit/integration tests and adhere to the documented API contracts."
    },
    {
      "id": "database-stubs",
      "title": "Create database stubs and schema mocks",
      "description": "Build in-memory database stubs, mock repositories, and schema fixtures to simulate persistence for unit tests and local integration.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "design-api-spec",
        "project-setup"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Database stubs emulate CRUD behavior per schema, are reusable in tests, and allow tests to run deterministically without a real DB."
    },
    {
      "id": "unit-tests",
      "title": "Write unit tests for auth and modules",
      "description": "Author unit tests for authentication, Module A, Module B, and API handlers using the database stubs and mocks; include edge and error cases.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-auth",
        "implement-module-a",
        "implement-module-b",
        "database-stubs"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "Automated tests cover critical paths (target coverage defined), run in CI, and all tests pass consistently using the stubs."
    },
    {
      "id": "integrate-and-deploy",
      "title": "Integrate modules and deploy to staging",
      "description": "Integrate Module A, Module B, auth, and API; run full test suite, deploy to staging, and perform end-to-end validation of the dashboard.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "unit-tests"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "Staging deployment completed, end-to-end acceptance tests pass, and dashboard features for modules A and B operate as intended under auth."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 8,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down functional decomposition: initialize project, define API contracts, implement cross-cutting auth, develop Module A and B in parallel, provide DB stubs for testing, then validate via unit tests and staging integration."
}
